title: neanotis hirsuta var. glabrior
intro: Neanotis hirsuta var. glabrior, also known as Indian snakeroot, is a plant species that belongs to the family Gentianaceae. Traditionally, Indian snakeroot has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating snake bites, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Indian snakeroot is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its potential to promote digestion and relieve various health conditions.
